title: Basal Ganglia (Basal Nuclei)
structures: substantia nigra (pars compacta and pars reticulata)
description: OpenStax coronal section diagram showing the basal nuclei (striatum, globus pallidus, subthalamic nucleus, substantia nigra) and their relationship to the thalamus and internal capsule, illustrating the direct and indirect pathways that modulate motor cortex output for smooth, coordinated movement.
clinical_notes: Parkinson's disease: dopaminergic neuron loss in substantia nigra → basal ganglia circuit dysregulation → bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor. OT role: handwriting adaptations (micrographia), fall prevention, freezing management, ADL strategies, visual cueing for gait.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 13 – The Anatomy of the Nervous System (Unit 3)
